title: MENLO PARK – Bold plans to speed clean energy action, waive fees for more San Mateo County community college students, and create a regional pharmacy using surplus meds to fill fre
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: Senate Bill 1203 sets a target for all state agencies to achieve net zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2035.
SB 1158 improves the way retail electricity suppliers report critical information, providing a more accurate picture of clean energy use in California.
SB 887 will help California swiftly build the transmission needed for zero-carbon electricity by 2045.
SB 1112 aims to support equitable financing to enable more people to afford decarbonization of their homes and buildings.
Studies show that incarcerated individuals who maintain contact with their loved ones are more successful upon reintegration into society.
Transitioning to an employee-owned model can enable small businesses to survive the retirement of their owners and build prosperity for stakeholders.
SB 1346 aims to address the issue of over $5 billion worth of unused medications being discarded by hospitals and long-term care facilities each year.
The high cost of pharmaceuticals forces nearly 1 in 4 Americans to skip doses or forgo filling prescriptions.
